title: rosa suavis var. engelmannii
intro: Rosa suavis var. engelmannii, also known as Engelmann's rose, is a species of fragrant flowering shrub that belongs to the rose family. The plant is native to the western United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Rosa suavis var. engelmannii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, and its essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
